_comment: Test case schema. One file per scenario. Fill in values when implementing eval.
description: Cooked chicken left at room temperature (~22C) for 12h, user asking about safety for next 24h
prompt: FILL IN: The opening user message that starts this scenario. Write it naturally, as a real user would type it. Do not mention all five slots upfront — let the agent elicit.

slot_ground_truth._comment: What the correct slot values are for this scenario. Used to evaluate elicitation accuracy.

synthetic_curve._comment: Used by model/inference.py stub when TEST_MODE=True. A plausible log CFU curve for this scenario. Length must be 100 (matches N_STEPS in config.py).

expected_output.reasoning_notes: FILL IN: 1-3 sentences describing what a correct response looks like. This is your rubric for manual eval. Write it before you run the model.
key_things_to_check: FILL IN: e.g. 'model correctly identifies rapid growth risk above 21C'
FILL IN: e.g. 'model communicates uncertainty across permutations'
FILL IN: e.g. 'model does not hedge so much that the safety conclusion is unclear'

eval_notes._comment: Free-form field. Write observations here after running the model against this case.
